ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION, 304, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF IS GRANTED UNDER C.C.P. 36(A). THE TRIAL DATE IS SEPTEMBER 8, 2025, AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 206. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 36(F) IS SEPTEMBER 17, 2025. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS SEPTEMBER 2, 2025. A FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR JULY 22, 2025, AT 4:00 P.M. A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 4, 2025, AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. ORDER SIGNED BY THE COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN. CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY. REPORTER: NOT REPORTED (304/EPS).

ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-20-2025 Set For Trial On Sep-08-25 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Jul-18-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar May-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2025, in Department 304, Line 2.

No appearances, not contested, The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. 36(a). Opposition filed by Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc. Joinder filed by Defendant Timec Services Company., Inc. Conditional non-opposition filed by Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company. The untimely joinder filed by Defendant Lone Star Industries, Inc., was not considered. Reply filed.
Defendants' oppositions and conditional demands are meritless. Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A. contends that the court should exercise its "discretion" to deny preference given the purported impracticability of bringing the case to trial within 120 days. Defendant is wrong. "Where a party meets the requisite standard for calendar preference under [Code of Civil Procedure section 36] subdivision (a), preference must be granted. No weighing of interests is involved." (Fox v. Superior Court (2018) 21 Cal.App.5th 529, 535; see also, e.g., Miller v. Superior Court (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 1200, 1204 [statute "grants a mandatory and absolute right to trial preference"]; Swaithes v. Superior Court (1989) 212 Cal.App.3d 1082, 1085 [trial court "has no power to balance the different interests of opposing litigants in applying the provision"]; Koch-Ash v. Superior Court (1986) 180 Cal.App.3d 689, 694 [section 36(a) "must be deemed to be mandatory and absolute" and "no discretion is left to trial courts."].)
Defendants also request that the court set the trial date no sooner than 120 days from the hearing and impose certain conditions on discovery and pre-trial motions. However, the court already adheres to the practice of scheduling trial dates as close to 120 days as possible. Moreover, the court will not impose conditions on Plaintiffs' right to a preferential trial date. In particular, it lacks authority to shorten the notice period for dispositive motions.

1. The trial date is September 8, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is September 17, 2025.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.
2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is September 2, 2025.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.
3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.
4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is August 22, 2025.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is September 5, 2025.
7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than June 20, 2025.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

(mini-minutes 1 of 2 continues, see next entry).

MOTION
FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE
IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND
EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (FOR TENTATIVE RULING PURPOSES ONLY)
Off Calendar May-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: (mini-minutes 2 of 2)

8. No later than June 3, 2025:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion.

9. A further case management conference is set for July 22, 2025, at 4:00 p.m.
a. Case management conference statements are not required.
b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom.
c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing.

10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than July 28, 2025. Response to the settlement demand is due August 11, 2025. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on August 18, 2025.

11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, September 4, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference.
Order signed by the court. Judge: Ethan P. Schulman. Clerk: Johnny Sengmany. Reporter: NOT REPORTED (304/EPS).
